mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and income expansion, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current payroll level 132,697k suggests ongoing labor market strength
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with payroll growth > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 1.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable below 2.5% year-over-year, the Fed is tightening into a low-inflation environment, signaling policy error risk and equity valuation compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.1% and CPIAUCSL at 227.505 indicate low baseline rates
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with low inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2% signals weakening consumer demand and forward earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 407,805M suggests baseline consumer activity
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains below 5%, indicating supply-side capacity expansion without labor constraints, supporting margin expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 95.9054 and UNRATE at 8.3% suggest room for supply expansion
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with slack labor market
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (INDPRO × UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,000k units while real GDP growth (GDPC1) slows below 2% annualized, signaling demand-side weakness that cascades through construction employment and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 699k and GDPC1 at 13,429.9B indicate moderate housing and growth levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or joint interaction Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% signals the Fed is behind the curve in tightening, creating inflation risk and equity volatility, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 8.3% and FEDFUNDS at 0.1% show loose monetary conditions
regime_hint: monetary policy lag in tight labor market
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contracts month-over-month by more than 0.3% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) remains flat or declining, signaling a supply-side shock with labor market deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM decline > 0.3% AND PAYEMS growth < 0.1% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 95.9054 and PAYEMS at 132,697k provide baseline for shock detection
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) grow month-over-month by more than 0.5%, indicating broad-based demand expansion that supports equity valuations and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 3.0% AND RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 13,429.9B and RSAFS at 407,805M establish baseline demand levels
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 62% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window during expansion regimes.
